log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable, tagged as a 'spreader' with an overlay. It's compiled in Visual Basic. Now I need to get all IOC types to understand the full scope of artifacts (IPs, domains, files,
The IOC summary shows no network activity (no IPs, domains, or HTTP), but 7 registry persistence keys, 2 processes created, and highlighted API calls including SetWindowsHookExA. The initial overview

Confirmed: no network activity (no C2, no DNS, no HTTP). This is a local-only dropper/spreader. I need to examine the dropped files to understand what payloads are being deployed. The initial overview

The dropped files tool returned empty, but the initial overview clearly listed files written: C:\DllLoader.exe, C:\Users\<USER>\AppData\Local\Temp\~DFBE4A107BFBF214C2.TMP, and C:\install.exe. This mig

Process activity confirms cmd.exe and reg.exe were spawned, with SetWindowsHookExA highlighted (consistent with the initial overview). The notable modules include system DLLs. I need to examine the re

The registry changes confirm a sophisticated dual-persistence technique: 1) IFEO hijacking of ZhuDongFangYu.exe, 360tray.exe, and taskmgr.exe using 'ntsd -d' debugger, and 2) File association hijackin
